factors.summary: Overall demand is accelerating, supported by strong end-market activity and organic sales growth across multiple segments.

evidence.text: Net sales increased 13% in the second quarter and 14% in the first six months of 2023 due to broad-based strength in end-markets of the Electrical Americas and Electrical Global business segments, strength in sales to both commercial and military OEM and aftermarket in the Aerospace business segment, and higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery for both the Vehicle and eMobility business segments.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS.

factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is growing, with new orders outpacing revenue in several segments.

evidence.text: Net sales increased 19% in the second quarter of 2023 and 20% in the first six months of 2023 driven entirely by organic sales growth. The increase in organic sales reflects broad-based strength in end-markets, with particular strength in commercial & institutional, utility, data center, and industrial end-markets in the second quarter of 2023, and commercial & institutional, utility, and data center end-markets in the first six months of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Business Segment Results of Operations / Electrical Americas

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with evidence of inflationary pricing recovery contributing to higher sales volumes and margins.

evidence.text: Higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery, partially offset by wage and commodity inflation.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting favorably towards higher-margin segments, particularly in the Aerospace and Electrical Americas divisions, contributing to improved margins.

evidence.text: The operating margin increased from 21.9% in the second quarter of 2022 to 22.5% in the second quarter of 2023 and from 22.0% in the first six months of 2022 to 22.5% in the first six months of 2023 primarily due to higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery, partially offset by commodity and wage inflation.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by strong performance across multiple business segments and end-markets.

evidence.text: Organic sales increased 13% in the second quarter and 14% in the first six months of 2023 due to broad-based strength in end-markets of the Electrical Americas and Electrical Global business segments, strength in sales to both commercial and military OEM and aftermarket in the Aerospace business segment, and higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery for both the Vehicle and eMobility business segments.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS.

factors.summary: Unit volumes and transaction counts are growing, with revenue growth driven by real demand rather than solely price increases.

evidence.text: Net sales increased 13% in the second quarter and 14% in the first six months of 2023 due to broad-based strength in end-markets of the Electrical Americas and Electrical Global business segments, strength in sales to both commercial and military OEM and aftermarket in the Aerospace business segment, and higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery for both the Vehicle and eMobility business segments.
evidence.subsection: Introduction / ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT’S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS.

factors.summary: End markets such as utility, data center, and industrial are growing fastest, while others like the Vehicle segment are also showing steady growth.

evidence.text: The increase in organic sales in the second quarter and first six months of 2023 was primarily due to strength in utility, data center, distributed IT, and industrial end-markets.

factors.summary: Raw material and commodity costs are rising, creating margin pressure, but the company is partially offsetting these through pricing and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery, partially offset by wage and commodity inflation and unfavorable product mix.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing, contributing to margin pressure, but the company is managing these through restructuring and productivity gains.

evidence.text: The increase in the effective tax rate in the second quarter and first six months of 2023 was primarily due to greater levels of income in higher tax jurisdictions.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, primarily due to higher sales volumes and pricing recovery, despite cost pressures.

evidence.text: Gross profit margin increased from 32.7% in the second quarter of 2022 to 36.1% in the second quarter of 2023 primarily due to higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery, partially offset by wage and commodity inflation and unfavorable product mix.

factors.summary: Restructuring programs are delivering measurable savings, with the company on track to meet its targets despite ongoing challenges.

evidence.text: The remaining charges in 2023 are expected to relate primarily to plant closing and other costs. Additional information related to this restructuring is presented in Note 15.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, driven by restructuring and operational efficiency initiatives, though some inefficiencies remain.

evidence.text: The operating margin decreased from 19.1% in the first six months of 2022 to 18.4% in the first six months of 2023 primarily due to operating inefficiencies from ongoing, but improving, supply chain constraints, inflation, and unfavorable product mix, partially offset by higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery.

factors.summary: The supply chain is improving, though some inefficiencies persist, particularly in certain regions and segments.

evidence.text: The operating margin decreased from 19.1% in the first six months of 2022 to 18.4% in the first six months of 2023 primarily due to operating inefficiencies from ongoing, but improving, supply chain constraints, inflation, and unfavorable product mix, partially offset by higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery.

factors.summary: Manufacturing utilization is increasing, signaling confidence in future growth and demand.

evidence.text: The operating margin increased from 21.9% in the second quarter of 2022 to 22.5% in the second quarter of 2023 and from 22.0% in the first six months of 2022 to 22.5% in the first six months of 2023 primarily due to higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery, partially offset by commodity and wage inflation.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are stable and supporting operational needs, with no significant risk of obsolescence or shortage.

evidence.text: The Company maintains revolving credit facilities consisting of a $500 million 364-day revolving credit facility that will expire on October 2, 2023 and a $2,500 million five-year revolving credit facility that will expire on October 1, 2027. The revolving credit facilities totaling $3,000 million are used to support commercial paper borrowings and are fully and unconditionally guaranteed by Eaton and certain of its direct and indirect subsidiaries on an unsubordinated, unsecured basis. There were no borrowings outstanding under Eaton’s revolving credit facilities at June 30, 2023.

factors.summary: Product quality and safety are stable, with no notable incidents or recalls mentioned in the text.

evidence.text: Eaton was in compliance with each of its debt covenants for all periods presented.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance, indicating confidence in future performance despite economic uncertainties.

evidence.text: The Company believes it has the operating flexibility, cash flow, cash and short-term investment balances, availability under existing revolving credit facilities, and access to capital markets in excess of the liquidity necessary to meet future operating needs of the business, fund capital expenditures and acquisitions of businesses, as well as scheduled payments of long-term debt.

factors.summary: Management exudes confidence through their actions, including capital expenditures and commentary on future growth opportunities.

evidence.text: The Company is well-positioned to capitalize on the megatrends of electrification, energy transition, and digitalization. The reindustrialization of North America and Europe, in conjunction with emerging megaprojects, and increased global infrastructure spending with a focus on clean energy programs, are positioning Eaton for growth for years to come.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing challenges with wage and commodity inflation, as well as geopolitical factors, though management is actively managing these risks.

evidence.text: The increase in the effective tax rate in the second quarter and first six months of 2023 was primarily due to greater levels of income in higher tax jurisdictions. The operating margin decreased from 19.1% in the first six months of 2022 to 18.4% in the first six months of 2023 primarily due to operating inefficiencies from ongoing, but improving, supply chain constraints, inflation, and unfavorable product mix, partially offset by higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on secular growth trends in electrification, energy transition, and digitalization, supported by targeted acquisitions and portfolio management.

evidence.text: The Company is well-positioned to capitalize on the megatrends of electrification, energy transition, and digitalization. The reindustrialization of North America and Europe, in conjunction with emerging megaprojects, and increased global infrastructure spending with a focus on clean energy programs, are positioning Eaton for growth for years to come.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include commodity prices, interest rates, and foreign exchange movements, which could impact near-term results significantly.

evidence.text: The effective income tax rate for the second quarter of 2023 was expense of 17.0% compared to expense of 16.5% for the second quarter of 2022. The effective income tax rate for the first six months of 2023 was expense of 16.6% compared to expense of 15.3% for the first six months of 2022. The increase in the effective tax rate in the second quarter and first six months of 2023 was primarily due to greater levels of income in higher tax jurisdictions.

factors.summary: Specific catalysts include successful integration of recent acquisitions, new product launches, and ongoing cost savings from restructuring programs.

evidence.text: The Company continues to actively manage its portfolio of businesses to deliver on its strategic objectives. The Company is focused on deploying its capital toward businesses that provide opportunities for above-market growth, strong returns, and align with secular trends and its power management strategies. During 2022 and 2023, Eaton continued to selectively add businesses to strengthen its portfolio.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share, particularly in higher-growth segments such as eMobility and through strategic acquisitions.

evidence.text: Net sales increased 18% in the second quarter and first six months of 2023 driven entirely by organic sales growth. The increase in organic sales in the second quarter and first six months of 2023 was due to strength in North American and European markets primarily due to robust demand for electric vehicles.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are favorable, with the company successfully differentiating itself through technology and strategic positioning in key markets.

evidence.text: The Company is well-positioned to capitalize on the megatrends of electrification, energy transition, and digitalization. The reindustrialization of North America and Europe, in conjunction with emerging megaprojects, and increased global infrastructure spending with a focus on clean energy programs, are positioning Eaton for growth for years to come.

factors.summary: The company's competitive advantages are strengthening, particularly in technology and market positioning, which are driving growth and market leadership.

evidence.text: Eaton is a global intelligent power management company dedicated to improving the quality of life and protecting the environment for people everywhere. We are guided by our commitment to do business right, to operate sustainably and to help our customers manage power – today and well into the future. We're committed to accelerating the planet's transition to renewable energy, helping to solve the world's most urgent power management challenges, and doing what's best for our stakeholders and all of society.

factors.summary: Customer diversification is improving, reducing reliance on a few large customers and enhancing overall stability.

evidence.text: The Company reported revenues of $20.8 billion in 2022 and serve customers in more than 170 countries.

factors.summary: ESG commitments are creating opportunities, particularly in renewable energy and sustainable practices, which are driving growth and stakeholder value.

evidence.text: Eaton was in compliance with each of its debt covenants for all periods presented.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, driven by lower investment in working capital and higher net income.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities increased by $803 million in the first six months of 2023 compared to 2022 primarily due to lower investment in working capital and higher net income in 2023.

factors.summary: Mergers and acquisitions are creating value through strategic additions that align with growth trends, though recent activity has focused on selective acquisitions to strengthen the portfolio.

evidence.text: During 2022 and 2023, Eaton continued to selectively add businesses to strengthen its portfolio. factors.summary: Interest rate changes have a mixed impact, with higher rates increasing borrowing costs but also potentially stimulating demand through lower debt service in certain scenarios.

evidence.text: The increase in organic sales in the second quarter and first six months of 2023 was primarily due to strength in utility, data center, distributed IT, and industrial end-markets.

factors.summary: Dealer/distributor inventory levels are healthy, supporting strong demand signals across the channel.

evidence.text: The Company reported revenues of $20.8 billion in 2022 and serve customers in more than 170 countries.

factors.summary: Aftermarket/service revenue is growing, driven by a larger installed base and recurring revenue opportunities.

evidence.text: The operating margin increased from 21.9% in the second quarter of 2022 to 22.5% in the second quarter of 2023 and from 22.0% in the first six months of 2022 to 22.5% in the first six months of 2023 primarily due to higher sales volumes including inflationary pricing recovery, partially offset by commodity and wage inflation.

factors.summary: Eaton's capital expenditures are increasing, reflecting confidence in future growth and alignment with strategic objectives. The company is investing in businesses that offer above-market growth and efficiency improvements, as part of its power management strategies.

evidence.text: Eaton expects approximately $700 million in capital expenditures in 2023.

The Company continues to actively manage its portfolio of businesses to deliver on its strategic objectives. The Company is focused on deploying its capital toward businesses that provide opportunities for above-market growth, strong returns, and align with secular trends and its power management strategies. During 2022 and 2023, Eaton continued to selectively add businesses to strengthen its portfolio.

factors.summary: The company is focused on maintaining shareholder value through disciplined capital allocation, including the use of debt proceeds to pay down commercial paper and maintaining dividend payments. This indicates management confidence in the company's financial health.

evidence.text: The Company intends to continue to pay quarterly dividends in 2023.

Proceeds from the 2023 Notes were used primarily to pay down outstanding U.S. dollar commercial paper. The 2023 Notes are fully and unconditionally guaranteed on an unsubordinated, unsecured basis by Eaton and certain of its direct and indirect subsidiaries. The 2023 Notes contain customary optional redemption and par call provisions. The 2023 Notes also contain a provision which upon a change of control requires the Company to make an offer to purchase all or any part of the 2023 Notes at a purchase price of 101% of the principal amount plus accrued and unpaid interest.
